# PyEDB-Core

{{ pyansys }} {{ python }} {{ pypi }} {{ MIT }}

{{ GH-CI }} {{ black }}

% reuse_start

PyEDB-Core is a Python client for the Electronics Database (EDB), a format for storing
information describing designs for Ansys Electronic Desktop (AEDT). Using the PyEDB-Core API,
you can make calls to an EDB server that is running either locally or remotely.

The EDB server can create, edit, read, and write EDB files to disk. These files can then be
read into AEDT and their designs simulated.

## Documentation and issues

Documentation for the latest stable release of PyEDB-Core is hosted at
[PyEDB-Core documentation](https://edb.core.docs.pyansys.com/version/stable/index.html#).
The documentation has five sections:

- [Getting started](https://edb.core.docs.pyansys.com/version/stable/getting_started/index.html#): Describes
  how to install PyEDB-Core in user mode.
- [User guide](https://edb.core.docs.pyansys.com/version/stable/user_guide/index.html): Describes how to
  use PyEDB-Core.
- [API reference](https://edb.core.docs.pyansys.com/version/stable/api/index.html): Provides API member descriptions
  and usage examples.
- [Examples](https://edb.core.docs.pyansys.com/version/stable/examples/index.html): Provides examples showing
  end-to-end workflows for using PyEDB-Core.
- [Contribute](https://edb.core.docs.pyansys.com/version/stable/contribute.html): Describes how to install
  PyEDB-Core in developer mode and how to contribute to this PyAnsys library.

In the upper right corner of the documentation's title bar, there is an option for switching from
viewing the documentation for the latest stable release to viewing the documentation for the
development version or previously released versions.

On the [PyEDB-Core Issues](https://github.com/ansys/pyedb-core/issues) page, you can create
issues to report bugs and request new features. When possible, use these issue templates:

- Bug report template
- Feature request template
- Documentation issue template
- Example request template

If your issue does not fit into one of these categories, create your own issue.

On the [Discussions](https://discuss.ansys.com/) page on the Ansys Developer portal, you can post questions,
share ideas, and get community feedback.

To reach the PyAnsys support team, email [pyansys.core@ansys.com](mailto:pyansys.core@ansys.com).

## License

PyEDB-Core is licensed under the MIT license.

PyEDB-Core makes no commercial claim over Ansys whatsoever. The use of this Python client requires
a legally licensed copy of AEDT. For more information, see the
[Ansys Electronics](https://www.ansys.com/products/electronics) page on the Ansys website.

documentation:
{class}`.BasicQuadraticParams`, {class}`.AdvancedQuadraticParams`: Quadratic model parameters of the thermal modifier.

The quadratic model is in this form: PropVal(Temp) = PropValRef[1 + C1(Temp - TempRef) + C2(Temp - TempRef)^2]

Create a material definition in a given database.

name

: Name of the material definition.

kwargs

: Dictionary to convert to a `MaterialDefPropertiesMessage` object.
  The dictionary key is the material property name. The dictionary value is the
  material property value. The expected keys for the kwargs are:

  - `permittivity`
  - `permeability`
  - `conductivity`
  - `dielectric_loss_tangent`
  - `magnetic_loss_tangent`
  - `thermal_conductivity`
  - `mass_density`
  - `specific_heat`
  - `youngs_modulus`
  - `poissons_ratio`
  - `thermal_expansion_coefficient`

# Returns

: Material definition object created.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.definition.padstack_def_data.PadstackDefData.get_pad_parameters
documentation:
Get pad parameters by layer name and pad type in their original values in the database.

# Parameters

layer

: Layer name.

pad_type

: Pad type.

# Returns

tuple\[{class}`PadGeometryType`, list of {class}`.Value`, {class}`.Value`, {class}`.Value`,
{class}`.Value`\]

or

tuple\[{class}`.PolygonData`, {class}`.Value`, {class}`.Value`, {class}`.Value`\]

> The tuple is in this format for other than polygons: `(pad_type, sizes, offset_x, offset_y, rotation)`.
>
> For polygons, the tuple is in this format: `(fp, offset_x, offset_y, rotation)`.
>
> - `pad_type`: Pad type
> - `sizes`: Pad parameters
> - `offset_x`: X offset
> - `offset_y`: Y offset
> - `rotation`: Rotation
> - `fp`: Polygon geometry

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.definition.padstack_def_data.PadstackDefData.set_pad_parameters
documentation:
Set pad parameters by layer name and pad type in their original values in the database.

# Parameters

layer

: Layer name.

pad_type

: Pad type.

offset_x

: X offset.

offset_y

: Y offset.

rotation

: Rotation.

type_geom

: Pad geometry type. The default is `None` if setting polygonal pad parameters.

sizes

: List of pad sizes. The default is `None` if setting polygonal pad parameters.

fp

: Polygon geometry. The default is `None` if not setting polygonal pad parameters.

documentation: Session manager for gRPC.

functions.name: ansys.edb.core.session.attach_session
documentation:
Attach a session to a port running the EDB API server.

# Parameters

ip_address

: IP address of the machine that is running the server. The default is `None`,
  in which case localhost is used.

port_num

: Port number that the server is listening on.

functions.name: ansys.edb.core.session.launch_session
documentation:
Launch a local session to an EDB API server.

The session must be manually disconnected after use by calling session.disconnect()

# Parameters

ansys_em_root

: Directory where the `EDB_RPC_Server.exe` file is installed.

port_num

: Port number to listen on. The default is `None`, in which
  case localhost is used.

# Examples

Create a session and then disconnect it

```pycon
>>> session = launch_session("C:\\Program Files\\AnsysEM\\v231\\Win64", 50051)
>>> # program goes here
>>> session.disconnect()
```

functions.name: ansys.edb.core.session.session
documentation:
Launch a local session to an EDB API server in a context manager.

# Parameters

ansys_em_root

: Directory where the `EDB_RPC_Server.exe` file is installed.

port_num

: Port number to listen on.

ip_address

: IP address where the server executable file is running. The default is `None`, in which
  case localhost is used.

  :::{note}
  This parameter is currently not supported. In future releases, this parameter is to
  support remotely running the API on another machine.
  :::

# Examples


modules.name: ansys.edb.core.inner.edb_logging
documentation:
Log module.

\## Objective
This module intends to create a general framework for logging.
This module is built upon the `logging` library and it does NOT intend to
replace it but rather provide a way to interact between `logging` and `EDB Client`.

# Usage

# Global logger

There is a global logger named `EDB_LOGGER`.
If you want to use this global logger, you must call it at the top of your module:

```from ansys.edb.core.utility import EDB_LOG

```

The default logging level of `LOG` is `ERROR`.
To change this and output lower-level messages, you can use this code:

```EDB_LOG.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

```

By default, this logger does not log to a file.
If you want to log to a file, can add a file handler using this code:

```import os file_path = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), 'edb_client.log') EDB_LOG.log_to_file(file_path)

```

The preceding code also sets the logger to be redirected to the file.

To log using this logger, call the desired method as a normal logger:

```import logging from ansys.edb.core.utility import edb_logging LOGGER = EDBLogger(level=logging.DEBUG, to_file=False, to_stdout=True) LOGGER.debug('This is LOG debug message.')
| DEBUG    | This is LOG debug message.
```

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.inner.variable_server.VariableServer.add_variable
documentation:
Add a variable.

# Parameters

name

: Variable name.

value

: Value, which can be any type that can be converted to a {class}`.Value`
  instance.

is_param

: Whether the new variable is a parameter. The default is `False`, which means it is a local variable.

# Notes

Variables can be added to the following EDB objects:

- {class}`.Database` (Variable names must begin with a '\$'.)
- {class}`.ComponentDef`
- {class}`.Cell`
- {class}`.Layout` (Adds variable to the corresponding
  {class}`.Cell` instance.)

# Examples

Add variables to a cell and create a value that references these variables.

```pycon
>>> param = Value(33.1)
>>> cell.add_variable("blue1", param)
>>> cell.add_variable("blue2", "25mm")
>>> vv = cell.create_value("blue1 + blue2")
>>> print(vv.double)
33.125
```

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.inner.base.TypeField.__get__
documentation: Get the static type if possible. Otherwise, fetch and return the instance type.

Provides a descriptor for a type field that can be overridden by subclasses.

You can have an optional `@property def _type(self)` in the same class as a fallback method to
fetch the type from the server in case the static type is unknown.

# Examples

class PrimitiveType(Enum):

: CIRCLE = 0

class Primitive(object):

: type = TypeField(None)
  @property
  def \_type(self):

  > return PrimitiveType(self.\_\_stub.GetPrimitiveType())

class Circle(Primitive):

: type = TypeField(PrimitiveType.CIRCLE)

Primitive(...).type # => fetch type from server.
Primitive.type # => None.
Circle(...).type # => CIRCLE.
Circle.type # => CIRCLE.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.layout.layout.Layout.expanded_extent
documentation:
Get an expanded polygon for a list of nets.

# Parameters

nets

: List of nets.

extent

: Geometry extent type for expansion.

expansion_factor

: Expansion factor for the polygon union. No expansion occurs if the value
  for this parameter is less than or equal to 0.

expansion_unitless

: When unitless, the distance by which the extent expands is the factor
  multiplied by the longer dimension (X or Y distance) of the expanded
  object/net.

use_round_corner

: Whether to use round corners or sharp corners. For round corners, this
  returns a bounding box if its area is within 10% of the rounded expansion's
  area.

num_increments

: Number of iterations desired to reach the full expansion.

# Returns

{class}`.PolygonData`

# Notes

This method returns the expansion of the contour, so any voids within expanded
objects are ignored.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.layout.layout.Layout.group_vias
documentation:
Create via groups from the primitives on the specified layer.

# Parameters

layer

: Layer containing the primitives to be grouped.

max_grouping_distance

: Maximum distance between vias in a via group .

persistent_vias

: Whether to preserve primitives during via group creation. If `False`
  primitives are deleted during via group creation.

group_by_proximity

: If `True`, vias are grouped by proximity (relative position to each other).
  If `False`, vias are grouped by range (any vias within the specified maximum
  grouping distance of each other are grouped)

check_containment

: If `True`, the connectivity of via groups is checked and enforced to prevent
  short circuits in geometry connecting to the via group. If false, vias are
  grouped regardless of the connectivity of touching geometry.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.layout.layout.Layout.snap_vias
documentation:
Snap vias on the specified layer to touching geometry.

# Parameters

layer

: Layer containing the vias to be snapped.

via_snapping_tol

: Tolerance for snapping vias. If snap_by_area_factor is `True`, this
  value should not have a unit.

prim_snapping_tol

: Tolerance for snapping primitives.

snap_by_area_factor

: If `True`, the via snapping tolerance is a factor of the surface area of the via.
  If `False`, the via snapping tolerance is treated as an absolute distance.

remove_dangling_vias

: If `True`, vias not connected to any geometry are removed.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.layout.layout.Layout.snap_primitives
documentation:
Snap primitives on the specified layer to touching geometry.

# Parameters

layer

: Layer containing the primitives to be snapped.

tol

: Tolerance for snapping primitives.

check_connectivity

: If `True`, the connectivity of primitives is checked and enforced to prevent
  short circuits in geometry connecting to the primitives. If false, primitives are
  grouped regardless of the connectivity of touching geometry.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.layout.layout.Layout.create_mesh_region
documentation:
Designate a mesh region in a design and create partitions for simulation.

# Parameters

xy_exp: {class}`.Value`

: Horizontal padding on both sides of the new mesh.

pos_z_exp: {class}`.Value`

: Vertical padding above the new mesh region.

neg_z_exp: {class}`.Value`

: Vertical padding below the new mesh region.

use_active_nets: bool

: True will create a new mesh region defined by the active nets in the design.
  False will create a new mesh region defined by the dielectric extents in the design.

incl_ref: bool

: True will include bot positive nets and reference nets in the definition of the new mesh region.
  False will not include bot positive nets and reference nets.

ext: {class}`.ExtentType`

: Geometry extent type.

num_x_partitions: int

: Number of partitions to create on x axis in the new mesh region.

num_y_partitions: int

: Number of partitions to create on y axis in the new mesh region.
Represents a layout.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.utility.value.Value.sqrt
documentation: Square root of this value as a constant value.

Represents a number or an expression.

# Attributes

val

: Value assigned to the new value.

\_owner

: {class}`.Cell`,
  {class}`.Layout`,
  {class}`.ComponentDef`

# Notes

A value can be either a constant (such as `1`, `2.35`, `"7+0.3i"`, and `"23mm"`) or
parametric (such as.\`\`w1 + w2\`\`).

If the value is parametric, the `_owner` attribute must be set to the object that hosts the
variables used. If the owner is {class}`.Cell` or
{class}`.Layout`, the expression can reference both database variables
and cell variables. A better way to create a parametric values is to call the
`obj_with_variables.create_value(str)` method, which automatically sets the
`_owner` parameter to the correct object.

Values can be used in expressions with the following operators:

```
.. list-table:: **Mathematical operators supported by values**
   :widths: 25 25 25
   :header-rows: 1

   * - Operator
     - Operation
     - Return Value
   * - \+
     - addition
     - Value
   * - \-
     - subtraction or negation
     - Value
   * - \*
     - multiplication
     - Value
   * - /
     - division
     - Value
   * - //
     - floor division
     - integer
   * - \*\*
     - power
     - Value
   * - ==
     - equality
     - bool
   * - \<
     - less than
     - bool
   * - >
     - greater than
     - bool
```

The value is evaluated to a constant (if it is parametric) before applying the operators.

classes.name: ansys.edb.core.utility.hfss_extent_info.HfssExtentInfo

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.utility.hfss_extent_info.HfssExtentInfo.__init__
documentation:
Create an HFSS extent information object.

# Parameters

use_open_region: bool, default: True

: Whether an open region is used.

extent_type: HfssExtentInfo.HFSSExtentInfoType, default: BOUNDING_BOX

: Extent type.

open_region_type: HfssExtentInfo.OpenRegionType, default: RADIATION

: Open region type.

base_polygon: Primitive, default: None

: Polygon to use if the extent is the `Polygon` type.

dielectric_extent_type: HfssExtentInfo.HFSSExtentInfoType, default: BOUNDING_BOX

: Dielectric extent type.

dielectric_base_polygon

: Polygon to use if dielectric extent is the `Polygon` type.

dielectric: (float, bool), default: (0, True)

: Dielectric extent size. The first parameter is the value.
  The second parameter is a Boolean indicating if the value is a multiple.

honor_user_dielectric: bool, default: True

: Whether to honor a user-defined dielectric primitive when calculating the dielectric
  extent.

airbox_truncate_at_ground: bool, default: False

: Whether to truncate the airbox at the ground layers.

airbox_horizontal: (float, bool), default: (0.15, True)

: Airbox horizontal extent size. The first parameter is the value.
  The second parameter is a Boolean indicating if the value is a multiple.

airbox_vertical_positive: (float, bool), default: (0.15, True)

: Airbox positive vertical extent size. The first parameter is the value.
  The second parameter is a Boolean indicating if the value is a multiple.

airbox_vertical_negative: (float, bool), default: (0.15, True)

: Airbox negative vertical extent size. The first parameter is the value.
  The second parameter is a Boolean indicating if the value is a multiple.

sync_airbox_vertical_extent: bool, default: True

: Whether to synchronize the airbox positive and negative vertical extent.

is_pml_visible: bool, default: False

: Whether to check to see if PML boxes are to be rendered.

operating_frequency

: PML operating frequency.

radiation_level

: PML radiation level for calculating the thickness of the boundary.

user_xy_data_extent_for_vertical_expansion: bool, default: True

: Whether to retain the old behavior for the vertical expansion of the airbox.
  The default is `True`, in which case the vertical extent is calculated from
  the XY data extent.

Represents mesh operations.

# Attributes

name

: Name of the mesh operation. The default is `""`, in which case a name is automatically assigned.

net_layer_info

: List with each entry having a net name, layer name, and `isSheet` flag indicating
  if the entry is a sheet.

enabled

: Whether the mesh operation is enabled.

refine_inside

: Whether to refine inside.

mesh_region

: Mesh region.

solve_inside

: Whether to solve inside.

    spatial domain of the design is queried.

list\[{class}`.LayoutObjInstance`\] or tuple\[list\[{class}`.LayoutObjInstance`\], list\[{class}`.LayoutObjInstance`\]\]

documentation: Initialize a sweep data setting.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.simulation_setup.simulation_setup.SweepData.frequency_string
documentation: {obj}`str`: String representing the frequency sweep data.

Class representing a sweep data setting.

# Attributes

name

: Name of this sweep.

distribution

: Sweep distribution type (see table below).

start_f

: Start frequency is number with optional frequency units.

end_f

: End frequency is number with optional frequency units.

step

: Step is either frequency with optional frequency units or an integer when a count is needed.

enabled

: True if this is enabled.

type

: Type of sweep.

use_q3d_for_dc

: Use Q3D solver for dc calculations.

save_fields

: Save fields during simulations.

save_rad_fields_only

: Save only radiation fields during simulations.

compute_dc_point

: Calculate DC point during simulations.

siwave_with_3dddm

: SIwave with 3D DDM.

use_hfss_solver_regions

: SIwave with HFSS solver regions.

use_hfss_solver_region_sch_gen

: SIwave with HFSS solver regions schematic.

use_hfss_solver_region_parallel_solve

: SIwave with HFSS solver parallel region simulation.

use_adp_solution_for_all_sweep_freq

: Enable Using Adaptive Solution for all frequency points.

num_parallel_hfss_regions

: SIwave with HFSS regions: number of regions to solve in parallel.

parallel_hfss_regions_sim_cfg

: List of CHFSSRegionComputeResource assignments for parallel region sims.

auto_s_mat_only_solve

: Auto/Manual SMatrix only solve.

min_freq_s_mat_only_solve

: Minimum frequency SMatrix only solve.

frequencies

: Frequency points in the frequency sweep.

steady_state_start

: Frequency of Steady State Start.

mesh_freq_choice

: Meshing frequencies of the observation mesh.

mesh_freq_points

: Frequency points in meshing frequencies.

mesh_freq_range_start

: Start meshing frequency.

mesh_freq_range_stop

: Stop meshing frequency.

interpolation_data

: Data for interpolating frequency sweeps.

# Notes

Here are the choices for the distribution parameter:

```
.. list-table:: Values for distribution parameter
   :widths: 20 45 25
   :header-rows: 1

   * - Distribution
     - Description
     - Example
   * - LIN
     - linear (start, stop, step)
     - LIN 2GHz 4GHz 100MHz or LIN 1dBm 10dBm 1dB
   * - LINC
     - linear (start, stop, count)
     - LINC 2GHz 4GHz 11
   * - ESTP
     - Exponential step (start, stop, count)
     - ESTP 2MHz 10MHz 3
   * - DEC
     - decade (start, stop, number of decades)
     - DEC 10KHz 10GHz 6
   * - OCT
     - octave (start, stop, number of octaves)
     - OCT 10MHz 160MHz 5
```

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.layout_instance.layout_instance_context.LayoutInstanceContext.placement_elevation
documentation:
{obj}`float`: Placement elevation of the context.

This parameter only applies if the context does not have 3D placement enabled.
If the context has 3D placement enabled, `None` is returned because the
placement of the context is dictated by the underlying 3D transformation.
Otherwise, the placement elevation of the context is returned.

This property is read-only.

methods.name: ansys.edb.core.layout_instance.layout_instance_context.LayoutInstanceContext.get_is_3d_placement
documentation:
Determine if the context has 3D placement enabled.

# Parameters

local

: Whether 3D placement is enabled only in the local {class}`context <LayoutInstanceContext>`.
  If `False`, a check is run to see if 3D placement is enabled anywhere higher in the
  hierarchy.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if 3D placement is enabled in the local context.
Represents the layout instance context object.
